Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is mildly impaired (slightly elevated serum creatinine), with hyperchloremia. Urinalysis shows proteinuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Low HbA1c suggests possible anemia or altered red cell turnover. Findings may indicate early renal dysfunction and possible dehydration or catabolic state; clinical correlation and further evaluation are recommended.
